title: Seeds, Origins, Paths, Query Space, Budgets, and Stop Policy · Behavior Case: Path Query Policy
summary: A seed expands to subdomains, downloads, calendars, search results, and logout links while the crawler has no page, byte, time, or incident limit. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the path query policy increment in bounded crawl-scope policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Express permitted paths, methods, query keys, values, pagination, fragments, and content types as testable admission rules.
Define allowed schemes, normalized hosts, ports, subdomains, redirects, and cross-origin resources with deny-by-default rules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern

Build deterministic Python evidence to validate seed identity, origin and path admission, resource budgets, and automatic and manual stop policy; personal data must remain minimized and redacted; then replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. A defensible solution applies Path Query Policy, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to bounded crawl-scope policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-crawl-scope-policy-5
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-crawl-scope-policy-5.
# Case 9036: a multilingual public-service site review.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it.
def worked_scraper_crawl_scope_policy_5(admitted_origins: int = 1, admitted_paths: int = 8, page_budget: int = 240, byte_budget: int = 12000000, stop_paths: int = 5) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "authorizationworked5",
        "url9036",
        "response5dekrow",
        "treeworked59036",
        "frontier9036worked5",
        "report5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if admitted_origins != 1:
        return False, admitted_origins, "origin-scope"
    if admitted_paths < 1:
        return False, admitted_paths, "path-policy"
    if page_budget < 1 or byte_budget < 1024:
        return False, page_budget, "resource-budget"
    if stop_paths < 3:
        return False, stop_paths, "stop-policy"
    assert admitted_origins == 1 and stop_paths >= 3
    return True, page_budget, "bounded-crawl-scope"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic fixed-fixture pure-Python crawler decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected authorization, URL, response, tree, record, frontier, browser, report, or recovery case, then state which network, owner, robots, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, accessibility, privacy, security, legal, load, schedule, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-path-query-policy-y6noc2
# Competency: scraper-path-query-policy.
# Case 5040: a multilingual public-service site review.
# Operating constraint: automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance.
# Changed case: introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it.
def evidence_scraper_path_query_policy_y6noc2(admitted_origins: int = 1, admitted_paths: int = 8, page_budget: int = 240, byte_budget: int = 12000000, stop_paths: int = 5) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_y6noc2 = "y6noc2-2con6y-5040"
    assert evidence_variant_y6noc2
    evidence_axes_y6noc2 = (
        "authorizationy6noc2",
        "url5040",
        "response2con6y",
        "treey6noc25040",
        "frontier5040y6noc2",
        "report2con6y5040",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_y6noc2)) == 6
    if admitted_origins != 1:
        return False, admitted_origins, "origin-scope"
    if admitted_paths < 1:
        return False, admitted_paths, "path-policy"
    if page_budget < 1 or byte_budget < 1024:
        return False, page_budget, "resource-budget"
    if stop_paths < 3:
        return False, stop_paths, "stop-policy"
    assert admitted_origins == 1 and stop_paths >= 3
    return True, page_budget, "bounded-crawl-scope"

lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Express permitted paths, methods, query keys, values, pagination, fragments, and content types as testable admission rules.

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Path Query Policy, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the path query policy defect evidence boundary.

For repair the path query policy defect, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Path Query Policy model · Theory Path Query Policy
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Path Query Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 1206 during a multilingual public-service site review. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate seed identity, origin and path admission, resource budgets, and automatic and manual stop policy; the target owner must be able to pause the crawl; then inject one retry, cancellation, queue, checkpoint, or output failure and recover cleanly. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Express permitted paths, methods, query keys, values, pagination, fragments, and content types as testable admission rules.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Path Query Policy model · Theory Path Query Policy.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Path Query Policy model · Theory Path Query Policy, keep Path Query Policy connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Express permitted paths, methods, query keys, values, pagination, fragments, and content types as testable admission rules.

hints: For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Path Query Policy model · Theory Path Query Policy, name the owner, authorization, stakeholder, revision, runtime, dependency, seed, URL, origin, robots, response, parser, selector, record, frontier, privacy, report, stop, recovery, and transfer boundary, Path Query Policy precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Path Query Policy model · Theory Path Query Policy, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, URL admission, robots, fetch, redirect, decode, parse, extract, provenance, schedule, report, rejection, stop, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory site-quality team handles case 1206 during a multilingual public-service site review. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate seed identity, origin and path admission, resource budgets, and automatic and manual stop policy; the target owner must be able to pause the crawl; then inject one retry, cancellation, queue, checkpoint, or output failure and recover cleanly. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Express permitted paths, methods, query keys, values, pagination, fragments, and content types as testable admission rules.

For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Path Query Policy model · Theory Path Query Policy, challenge the documented misconception with changed-authorization, URL, robots rule, response, encoding, markup, selector, frontier, timing, session, browser, privacy, security, export, failure, recovery, and controlled owner transfer evidence: Following every same-host href keeps a crawler in scope.
